resposta:
```python
class Solution:
    def maximumXOR(self, nums: List[int]) -> int:
        result = 0
        for num in nums:
            result ^= num
        return result
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution utilizes the XOR operation's property that a ^ a = 0 and a ^ 0 = a, so any number XOR itself will cancel out, and any number XOR 0 will remain unchanged. The maximum possible bitwise XOR of all elements of nums after applying the operation any number of times is equivalent to the bitwise XOR of all elements of nums, as the operation can only decrease the value of each element.
